spouse.name: Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny

km.France: ប្រទេសបារាំង

zh.lifeSummary: Avoye de Sainte Maure出生在法國。她於1205年之前與Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Avoye de Sainte Maure出生在法国。她于1205年之前与Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sainte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Sain   1, a surname of German origin.
French: probably a variant of Saïn (see Sain 6).
West African (Gambia): variant of the Serer name Sène (see Sene 2).

variants.name: Sain
meanings: Americanized form of German Zirn, which is a variant of Zürn (see Zurn ). Compare Saine , Sane , and Sayne .

Croatian and Serbian (Šain): metonymic occupational name for a hawker or a nickname from ša(h)in ‘falcon, hawk (trained for hunting)’, a word of Turkish and ultimately Persian origin (see Sahin ). Compare Shain and Shine .

Americanized form of Slovenian and Croatian Šajn: nickname from šajn, an obsolete loanword from German meaning ‘shine’, or a Slavicized form of German Schein . As a Croatian surname it may also be a variant of Šain (see 2 above). Compare Shain and Shine .

variants.name: Sene
meanings: French (Sené and Séné): nickname from Old French sené ‘wise, sensible’.

West African (Senegal; Sène): name found among the Serer people, of unexplained etymology. The same name forms part of the name of Roog Seen, the supreme deity in the indigenous Serer religion. Compare Saine 3.

nameMeanings.name: Maure
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Mauri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ernesto, Ramon, Adela, Carlos, Eladio, Emilia, Estrella, Horacio, Jorge, Jose, Juan, Mario.  Italian Alessandra, Angelo, Cecilio, Cosimo, Gianfranco, Lorenzo, Mauro.
Catalan: from the personal name Mauri, Latin Maurus (see Mauro ).
Catalan and French (Occitan): probably from the personal name Malric (ancient Germanic Almaricus).

variants.name: Mauriello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Flavio, Salvatore, Alfonse, Attilio, Carmine, Gino, Nicola, Nunzio, Sal, Vicenzo.
Italian (mainly southern): from a pet form of   Mauro  .

variants.name: Maurin
meanings: French: from a medieval personal name derived from the Latin name Maurinus, a derivative of Maurus ‘Moor’ (see Mauro ), which was the name of a 6th-century Christian martyr. This form of the surname is also found in Germany, Italy, and Slovenia (see below). Compare Morin .

Germanized or Americanized form of Slovenian Mavrin: from a personal name derived from the Latin name Maurinus (see above). In part, Maurin is a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname, originating from the Kočevsko region in Lower Carniola, Slovenia (see Kocevar ).
Italian: rare variant of   Maurino  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Morel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ana, Luis, Francisco, Juana, Rafael, Ramon, Jorge, Juan, Julio, Miguel, Pedro.  French Armand, Andre, Franck, Francois, Francoise, Marcel, Normand, Pierre, Alphonse, Benoit, Camille, Cecile.
French and Spanish: from the medieval personal name Morel, from the Latin name Maurellus, derived from Maurus ‘Moor’ (see Mauro , compare Moreau 1).
French: nickname for a dark-skinned man, from Old French morel ‘dark brown, black’, a diminutive of moré ‘brown, black’ (see More 2, compare Moreau 2). Compare also Morell 3 and Morrell 2.

variants.name: Maura
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Eduardo, Humberto, Amada, Amalia, Armando, Bernardo, Carlos, Jesus, Jose, Juan, Luis.  Italian Antonio, Giuseppe, Rino, Romolo, Vincenzo.
Catalan and French: variant of Maure, from the Latin personal name Maurus (see Mauro ).
Italian (southern): from the feminine form of the personal name Mauro , Latin Maurus.

variants.name: Santomauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Marco, Ornella, Pietro, Salvatore, Silvano, Vincenza.
Italian: habitational name from any of numerous places called with reference to a local church or shrine dedicated to a Saint Maurus, San(to) Mauro in Italian (see Mauro ).

variants.name: Moreau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Jacques, Armand, Lucien, Marcel, Normand, Gaston, Laurent, Michel, Ovide, Philippe.
French and Walloon:
from the medieval personal name Morel, from the Latin name Maurellus, derived from Maurus ‘Moor’ (see Mauro , compare Morel 1).

en.lifeSummary: Avoye de Sainte Maure was born in France. She married Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny before 1205. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Avoye de Sainte Maure est née à France. Elle a épousé Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny avant 1205. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Avoye de Sainte Maure wurde in Frankreich geboren. Sie hat vor 1205 Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Avoye de Sainte Maure è nata il nel Francia. Lei sposato Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny prima del 1205. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Avoye de Sainte Maureはフランスで生まれた。1205年以前にGuillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précignyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Avoye de Sainte Maure(은)는 프랑스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1205년 이전 Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Avoye de Sainte Maure nasceu em França. Ela casou-se com Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny antes 1205. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Avoye de Sainte Maure родилась в местности Франция. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny перед 1205 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Avoye de Sainte Maure nació en Francia. Se casó con Guillaume de Sainte Maure et de Précigny antes de 1205. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.

th.–: ถึงแก่กรรม
th.–Female: ถึงแก่กรรม
